name: requirements-interview
use_when: Clarify an ambiguous idea before planning or implementation.
asks: What decision or artifact should the clarified requirements support?
What is the highest-impact unknown?
checks: One blocking question at a time
Success conditions explicit before implementation
Assumptions recorded instead of hidden

name: plan-consensus
use_when: Plan broad architecture, large refactors, or work where critique can materially change the approach.
asks: What decision must the final plan settle?
What constraints or compatibility boundaries matter?
checks: Planner, Architect, Critic, and Final stages represented
Rejected alternatives tied to concrete tradeoffs
Final plan ready for implementation

name: goal-checkpoint
use_when: Break a long run into 2-6 current-run goals with explicit completion evidence.
asks: What final outcome should the goal list prove?
What evidence marks each goal complete?
checks: .tink/current/goals.json created when selected
Exactly one active goal while work is underway
Blocked goals include the smallest unblock action

name: delegation-brief
use_when: Prepare safe parallel work or handoff briefs without starting workers automatically.
asks: What work can be split without conflicts?
What evidence should each worker return?
checks: .tink/current/delegation.md created when selected
No tmux panes, worktrees, or workers started automatically
Packet scopes and forbidden actions visible

name: issue-triage
use_when: Sort incoming issues, external PRs, QA reports, or broad plans into durable states, ready-for-agent briefs, or vertical issue slices.
asks: What source should be triaged: issue, PR, QA report, plan, or conversation?
What outcome is wanted: needs-info, ready-for-agent, ready-for-human, wontfix, or new issue slices?
checks: Exactly one primary state or an explicit conflict note
Ready-for-agent work includes acceptance criteria and verification evidence
Slices are vertical and independently verifiable

name: bug-diagnosis-loop
use_when: Diagnose hard bugs, regressions, intermittent failures, or performance problems by building a red-capable feedback loop before changing code.
asks: What exact symptom must the loop catch?
What command, test, script, trace, or manual action currently reproduces it?
checks: A named loop can catch the exact symptom or is explicitly blocked
The minimized repro still represents the user's bug
The focused loop is checked before broader verification

name: review-two-axis
use_when: Review a branch, PR, or diff since a fixed point along separate Standards and Spec axes.
asks: What is the fixed point: commit, branch, tag, PR, or merge-base?
What spec, issue, PRD, or user request should the diff satisfy?
checks: The fixed point resolves and the diff is non-empty
Standards and Spec are reported separately
Each finding cites evidence and impact

name: decision-map
use_when: Map loose ideas with multiple unresolved decisions into ordered research, prototype, or discussion tickets across sessions.
asks: What final decision or plan should the map unlock?
Which unknown is the current frontier?
checks: The map has a clear finish line and current frontier
Each ticket is sized for one session and has a type
Resolved tickets include evidence links

name: architecture-deepening
use_when: Find and plan codebase architecture improvements around deep modules, interfaces, seams, leverage, locality, and testability.
asks: Which area or module should be reviewed?
What pain matters most: testability, navigation, coupling, duplication, or interface complexity?
checks: Candidates use domain language and architecture vocabulary
Each candidate explains leverage, locality, and test impact
The final plan verifies at the chosen seam

name: loop-engineering
use_when: User explicitly wants to iterate act-observe-evaluate-fix against a measurable acceptance signal until it passes or a budget is hit. For 'make X pass / reach quality bar Y' goals. Not for ordinary multi-step work (goal-checkpoint) or hard-bug diagnosis (bug-diagnosis-loop).
asks: What single acceptance signal judges each iteration?
What is the iteration budget before stopping to report?
What evaluates results independently of the change?
checks: One bottleneck fixed per iteration
Acceptance signal is runnable, not self-judged
Each iteration logged before the next change
Budget respected; stop-and-report instead of infinite loop

name: ship
use_when: Prepare a PR, release, deployment, or public handoff.
asks: What is shipping?
What rollback or risk matters?
checks: CI checks pass or skip reason stated
Rollback documented
Known risks explicit

name: harness-synthesis
use_when: Create a domain-specific harness from research, failures, user feedback, or repeated work when generic harnesses are not enough.
asks: What recurring task or failure should improve?
What research or examples should it learn from?
checks: Specific and non-duplicative
Operational checks and done criteria
Saved only after approval

name: harness-curation
use_when: Choose, replace, synthesize, weave, or frog harnesses when too many tools or heavy workflows would hurt the current task. Also use when context or prompt habits need calibration across runs.
asks: What is the current task and success evidence?
What constraint makes heavy harnesses risky?
checks: Smallest effective set chosen
Heavy harnesses rejected or deferred with reason
Maintenance saved only after approval

name: pre-publish-multi-agent-verify
use_when: Verify a public publish/release across multiple isolated environments with parallel agents — install matrix, UX walkthrough, doc consistency, leak audit, slash/contract coverage. Use when generic ship checks miss broad surface (npm/PyPI/GitHub Release/Docker Hub).
asks: What package/artifact ships to which medium?
What 2-4 user-target scenarios should agents share as the verification target?
What isolation mechanism (git worktree, temp dir, container)?
checks: Build artifact produced and recorded
User-target scenarios locked before agents start
Each agent owns one concern, ≤300 words, evidence-only
Findings classified blocker/major/minor/nit
CHANGELOG [Unreleased] merged into the shipping version
No secrets, credentials, or private paths in published files
Advertised commands/API present in shipped artifact (sha 1:1)

name: pr-merge
use_when: Merge a GitHub PR after verifying CI, diff, and conflicts. Use when a PR is ready to merge.
asks: Which PR? (number, URL, or current branch)
Merge strategy: squash, merge, or rebase?
Any known risks or rollback plan?
checks: CI all green or skip reason approved
No conflicts (mergeable: MERGEABLE)
Diff scope matches PR description
Rollback base SHA recorded

name: tink-feedback-apply
use_when: User gives feedback about Tink's behavior, interaction pattern, or output quality — classify the feedback layer and apply the minimum fix.
asks: What exactly went wrong or felt wrong?
Should this fix persist for future sessions?
checks: Correct layer identified
Minimum viable change only
Triggering scenario verified

name: ko-tech-doc-audit
use_when: Audit Korean technical document content completeness — find empty claims, missing evidence, unactionable sentences, missing verification, and missing failure conditions. Use when the document reads well but leaves no information behind. Persona (reviewer perspective) is injectable at cast time.
asks: Which document should be audited?
Which reviewer persona should be applied? (default: Korean technical document editor)
Should the output include a revised version, or findings only?
checks: Every key claim linked to evidence, procedure, verification, or failure condition
Empty claims, unactionable sentences, missing verification, missing failure conditions listed
No facts added that are absent from the original
Persona does not weaken acceptance criteria
